titleStmt.title: A Transcription of Codex Sangallensis 60
VL47
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

handDesc.p: Written in Irish majuscule and minuscule.

decoDesc.p: Black ink with rubrication. Decorated pages with black, yellow and red ink.

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers the whole manuscript. It has been identified as Old Latin in John 1:29-3:26, although there are also non-Vulgate readings elsewhere, including the heavily corrected opening pages.The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation (rendered by mid-dots) and capitalisation. Chapter numbers in the margin have also been recorded. All abbreviations are reproduced apart from the symbols for 'autem', 'enim' and 'est'. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: Whole page picture of the evangelist, holding a book in which is written 'iohannis'.
The first two words are written as a stylized display page.
The last two letters of 'gratiam' may have been corrected.
The chapter number referring to 1:19 is written in the left margin, in black ink (unlike the other chapter numbers), between two sets of Ammonian sections.
'frater' is written by the first hand in blank space at the end of the line above.
bethesda from Mizzi, who is tentative
The first hand has written a ligatured form of 'fici', and a corrector has erased the top of the 'c' to give 'fiu'.
'et dixit' is in a darker ink, but may be by the first hand.
There is a cross over 'faciebat' which may be a lectionary indication, although elsewhere it functions as a deletion mark (e.g. page 20).
There is a cross over 'quia' which may be a lectionary indication, although elsewhere it functions as a deletion mark (e.g. page 20).
sic
sic
The chapter number referring to 3:22 is written in the left margin.
There is a cross over 'profectus' which may be a lectionary indication, although elsewhere it functions as a deletion mark (e.g. page 20).
The chapter number referring to 4:1 is written in the left margin.
Double transcriptions reconciled to this point. From now on · JB transcription proofread against images
There is a cross over 'quam' which may be a lectionary indication, although elsewhere it functions as a deletion mark (e.g. page 20).
sic
The chapter number referring to 5:1 is written in the left margin.
sic
There is a cross over 'caritatem', which in this case may be a mark of deletion.
The chapter number referring to 6:1 is written in the left margin.
From 'Cum' to 'ememus' is written in a different hand.
From 'Cum' to 'ememus' is written in a different hand.
'eit' is clearly a misreading of 'est' and so has been included in verse 9.
The deleted mark is an Ammonian section number; see also line 9 below.
There is a cross over didicit.
The readings of the first hand and corrector may be reversed.
The 'q' of 'quia' is formed in an unusual way, like a 'z'.
There is a cross above 'mediante'.
There is a hole in the parchment at this point, extending over 3 lines.
The last 9 characters are written on the line above.
There is a hole in the parchment extending over 3 lines.
'e' followed by the abbreviation for 'enim'.
Another unusual form of 'q' (cf. John 7:11).
The chapter number referring to 8:21 is written in the left margin.
There is a cross above 'quia'.
'nis' is written by the first hand in blank space at the end of the line above.
'bant ergo' is written by the first hand in blank space at the end of the line above.
There is a cross over 'autem'.
There is a tear in the parchment.
There is a tail on the 'i' which may be an abbreviation for 'quia'.
There is a faint cross over 'lapides'.
There is a cross over 'et'.
There is a faint cross over 'donec'.
Word division sic.
There is a cross over 'audierunt'.
The chapter number referring to 10:1 is written in the left margin.
There is a cross above 'facta'.
There is a cross over 'Hoc'.
The chapter number referring to 11:55 is written in the left margin.
There is a cross over 'dederant'.
There is a cross above 'nardi'.
There is a cross above 'hominis'.
The chapter number referring to 13:1 is written in the left margin.
The word division is 'sede Inde'.
There is a cross over 'uobis'.
There are marks under 'in', but it is not clear whether these stem from a correction.
Abbreviation for quia followed by 'a'.
The chapter number referring to 15:1 is written in the left margin.
There is a cross over 'uobis'.
There is a cross over 'excussio'.
There is a cross over 'eis'.
There is a cross over 'habiero'.
There is a cross over 'loquitur'.
There is a cross over 'quis'.
There is a cross over 'crediderunt'.
The space may be an erasure.
There is a cross over 'si'.
'debat eu¬' is written on the end of the preceding line.
Hole in parchment extending over 6 lines.
Hole in parchment extending over 6 lines.
There is a cross over 'caedis'.
The chapter number referring to 18:28 is written in the left margin.
The original reading has suffered water damage and a corrector has added it at the end of the previous line.
There is a cross above 'gabbatha'.
There is a cross above 'sicut'.
There is a cross over 'paratim'.
Gap probably due to tear in parchment.
There are nine letters in the margin written by a later hand; part of a more substantial gloss or chapter title which has been cut off.
